word_list.word: a danger foreseen is half avoided
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dˈeɪndʒɚ foːɹsˈiːn ɪz hˈæf ɐvˈɔɪdᵻd

senses.definition: used to imply that if one anticipates a potential danger or problem, one can take steps to reduce the risk and potential harm

word_list.word: catch not at the shadow and lose the substance
word_list.pronunciation: kˈætʃ nˌɑːt æt ðə ʃˈædoʊ ænd lˈuːz ðə sˈʌbstəns

senses.definition: used to emphasize that one should not become distracted by superficial or insignificant details and lose sight of what is truly important or valuable

word_list.word: failing to plan is planning to fail
word_list.pronunciation: fˈeɪlɪŋ tə plˈæn ɪz plˈænɪŋ tə fˈeɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that without proper planning, one is more likely to encounter failures or setbacks

word_list.word: life is too short to do something that does not matter
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ɪz tˈuː ʃˈɔːɹt tə dˈuː sˈʌmθɪŋ ðæt dʌznˌɑːt mˈæɾɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that time is a precious and limited resource, and should be used wisely on things that truly matter

word_list.word: {not} empty the water jar until the rain falls
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ˈɛmpti ðə wˈɔːɾɚ dʒˈɑːɹ ʌntˈɪl ðə ɹˈeɪn fˈɔːlz

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not use up all of one's resources before they can be refilled or replaced

examples.x: The student did not spend all his savings until he was sure he could afford the tuition for the next semester, following the advice of the saying 'never empty the water jar until the rain falls.'

word_list.word: think much, speak little, and write less
word_list.pronunciation: θˈɪŋk mˈʌtʃ spˈiːk lˈɪɾəl ænd ɹˈaɪt lˈɛs

senses.definition: used to imply that one should think deeply and carefully before speaking or writing, emphasizing the importance of reflection and restraint in communication

word_list.word: {not} throw out your dirty water until you get in fresh
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt θɹˈoʊ ˈaʊt jʊɹ dˈɜːɾi wˈɔːɾɚɹ ʌntˈɪl juː ɡɛt ɪn fɹˈɛʃ

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should not discard or dispose of something that they currently have until they have found a suitable replacement or something of greater value

examples.x: The employee did not quit his job until he had secured a better one, believing in the saying 'never throw out your dirty water until you get in fresh.'

senses.definition: used to imply that taking the time to double-check measurements and details before proceeding can prevent mistakes and save time and resources in the long run

word_list.word: a stitch in time saves nine
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ stˈɪtʃ ɪn tˈaɪm sˈeɪvz nˈaɪn

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to address a problem or make a small repair early on, rather than waiting and allowing the problem to become more serious and difficult to fix later

examples.x: Fix that leak now, because a stitch in time saves nine.
A stitch in time saves nine, so it's better to address small issues at work before they escalate.
A small repair now will save a lot of time later; remember, a stitch in time saves nine.
Don't ignore your health; a stitch in time saves nine when it comes to avoiding illness.
If you study a little every day, a stitch in time saves nine when the exam approaches.
